channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Built a Free Daily AI News Engine Using Claude Code CLI — No API Key Needed
item.description: Every morning at 8:30am, my Mac writes four 1,200-word AI news articles — Bloomberg-style, with citations, analyst quotes, and structured headings — then commits them to GitHub and deploys to...

item.title: Att uppfinna hjulet
item.description: Vi skulle bygga en ny bilmodell. Något som verkligen stack ut. Något som signalerade innovation och mod. En tydlig identitet! Vi kom till steget där vi skulle bestämma hjul och däck. Vi funderade:...

item.title: Move Fast and Break Things Was Fun. Now We’re Paying For It.
item.description: The most valuable engineers I've worked with often looked... less "productive". They weren't always the fastest at closing tickets. They didn't push the most code. Sometimes it even felt like they...

item.title: I Built a Multi-Agent Coding System From Scratch in Python (No Frameworks)
item.description: Most multi-agent AI tutorials hand you LangChain, AutoGen, or CrewAI and say “here you go.” You wire a few abstractions together, get something running, and never really understand what’s...

item.title: Docker Compose in Production in 2026: I Ran My Real Stack for 30 Days and Here Are the Numbers
item.description: Docker Compose in Production in 2026: I Ran My Real Stack for 30 Days and Here Are the Numbers A docker-compose.yml in production is basically the neighborhood mechanic shop. No dealer-level...

item.title: Docker Compose en producción en 2026: corrí mi stack real durante 30 días y estos son los números
item.description: Docker Compose en producción en 2026: corrí mi stack real durante 30 días y estos son los números Un docker-compose.yml en producción es básicamente como el taller mecánico del barrio. No tiene la...

item.title: What I Learned Building HiyokoBar — A Menubar App That Does One Thing Per Click
item.description: If this is useful, a ❤️ helps others find it. All tests run on an 8-year-old MacBook Air. HiyokoBar is a menubar app. Click the icon — panel appears. Do the thing. Click away — panel disappears....

item.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, all your M&A questions will be answered
item.description: Leaders from Coinbase, M13, and Mignano Law Group talk about how M&A is an early-stage strategy at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Register to hear this live.

item.title: The Frankenstein Meeting Room: How to Stitch Angular, React, and Svelte Into One App
item.description: Part 1 of a series. The build follows in subsequent posts. Three frontend frameworks in the same business domain is the rule, not the exception. One team adopted Angular years ago. Another fell in...

item.title: The 10 free books I recommend to every engineer learning ML math (and why order matters)
item.description: After teaching hundreds of engineers learn machine learning last 5 years, a pattern becomes hard to ignore. Most people don’t struggle because machine learning is too difficult. struggle because...

item.title: I wrote a 104-page book on the GitHub signals that predict Series A rounds — free download
item.description: Three weeks ago I started turning the methodology behind GitDealFlow into a proper trade book. It shipped today. 104 pages, free PDF and EPUB, also fully readable on the open web. Here is what is...

item.title: 3 days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Three days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to Disrupt 2026. Buy one TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket, and get a second ticket at 50% off. Gain more visibility in the tech industry. Offer...

item.title: Blink bumps its budget buzzer to 2K
item.description: Amazon-owned Blink has two new video doorbells, the Blink Wired Doorbell 2K+ and the Blink Battery Doorbell 2K+. The wired doorbell is listed at $49.99 on Amazon, but a blog post from the company...

item.title: AI boom pushes Samsung to $1T
item.description: Samsung crossed the $1 trillion valuation mark after shares surged on AI-driven chip demand, making it only the second Asian company after TSMC to hit the milestone.

item.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
item.description: A celebration of the tweaks and customizations that make life easier at the CLI.

item.title: Google’s AI search summaries will now quote Reddit
item.description: Google is updating its AI Search features to make it easier for users to find information from sources they know and trust. One of the more notable changes introduces "a preview of perspectives"...

item.title: It’s time to let my iPhone Mini go
item.description: It's not the Mini, it's me. Nearly five years after it launched, the 13 Mini is still the best phone ever made. That hasn't changed; it's just that I changed. And as painful as it is to admit...

item.title: MuddyWater Uses Microsoft Teams to Steal Credentials in False Flag Ransomware Attack
item.description: The Iranian state-sponsored hacking group known as MuddyWater (aka Mango Sandstorm, Seedworm, and Static Kitten) has been attributed to a ransomware attack in what has been described as a "false...

item.title: The Download: seafloor science and military chatbots
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke...

item.title: The Hacker News Launches 'Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026' — Submissions Now Open
item.description: For nearly 20 years, we at The Hacker News have mostly told scary stories about cyberspace — big hacks, broken systems, and new threats. But behind every headline, there’s a quieter, better story....

item.title: The Remarkable Paper Pure is the best digital notepad I’ve ever used
item.description: Was I writing with pen and paper, or a stylus on a tablet? It’s getting more difficult than ever to tell. The Remarkable Paper Pure is a follow-up to 2020’s Remarkable 2 and a couple of premium...

item.title: Remarkable’s next E Ink digital notepad skips the color screen
item.description: Remarkable announced a new paper tablet today - albeit one without the advanced color screen found in its recent Paper Pro and Paper Pro Move tablets. The new Remarkable Paper Pure instead...

item.title: Energizer’s new coin batteries won’t cause ingestion burns if swallowed
item.description: Energizer has announced a new line of lithium coin batteries it claims are the world's first to eliminate the risk of ingestion burns if swallowed. Its Ultimate Child Shield line includes three...

item.title: Microsoft’s Office and LinkedIn chief now runs Teams in latest reshuffle
item.description: Microsoft's LinkedIn chief, Ryan Roslansky, took on an expanded role at the company as head of Office last year, and he's now getting more responsibilities as part of the latest leadership...

item.title: The clippening
item.description: Earlier this year, after a tumultuous period serving as the former second-in-command at the FBI, Dan Bongino went back to what he is perhaps known best for: video podcasting. After Bongino exited...

item.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
item.description: Analysts recently confirmed what identity security teams have quietly feared: AI agents are being deployed faster than enterprises can govern them. In their inaugural Market Guide for Guardian...

item.title: Apple Will Pay $250 Million to Settle Lawsuit Over Siri's AI Features
item.description: If you bought an iPhone 15 or 16 in the US, you could be set to pocket up to $95 per device as part of the settlement.

item.title: Rowhammer Attack Against NVIDIA Chips
item.description: A new rowhammer attack gives complete control of NVIDIA CPUs. On Thursday, two research teams, working independently of each other, demonstrated attacks against two cards from Nvidia’s Ampere...

item.title: Chrome’s AI features may be hogging 4GB of your computer storage
item.description: Google Chrome may be taking up more of your storage than expected thanks to a large on-device AI model file that, in some cases, is being automatically downloaded to the browser's system folders....

item.title: Hasan Piker, Self-Described ‘Ayatollah of Woke,’ Wants AI to Die
item.description: The far-left Twitch streamer says AI is rotting our brains. He’s also addicted to Twitter and listens to at least eight podcasts.
